other_observers: none; Martin Wolf found the bird on the 15th of December on the Colorado Springs CBC.
specific_location: On Willow Circle near its intersection with Cheyenne Blvd about a quarter mile below Starsmore Discovery Center at the base of North Cheyenne Canyon.
description: Woodpecker by chisel-like bill. All black on back. Face with characteristic white iris surrounded by a black mask that extends to nape. Top of head with three colors making it a female. Black behind bill base, then wide band of black and then a smaller area of red before becoming black on the nape and back. Breast black with white belly with thin verticle streaks. Throat black surrounded by white.Behaviors: 1) foraged for over 15 minutes at on the trunck of an old silver maple at a large area near where an old branch might have been. It was pecking and one photo shows the bird's tongue extended indicating that is was searcing for under bark goodies.   2) Also, I observed it fly to a wooden suet feeder with 2" diameter holes filled with a suet/seed mixture. The bird fed there for about 3 minutes.Call: heard a "woka-woka-woka" on two occasions otherwise silentPlumage: presumably adult
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
